commonIssues:
Errors in documents, such as variations in name spelling between Aadhaar cards and affidavits, lead to immediate rejection.

How to fix:

  • Update your Aadhaar card to match your 10th marksheet or affidavit before starting the application.

Frequent failures in Aadhaar-based authentication (OTP not received, biometric failures) stop the application process.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the mobile number linked to your Aadhaar is active. Visit an e-Mitra or Aadhaar center if biometric fails.

A common glitch on the SSO portal where a user cannot register because their number is linked to another record.

How to fix:

  • Use a unique mobile number for registration on the Pehchan Portal or SSO portal.

The system failing to recognize or show the member's name in the family list requires prior updating.

How to fix:

  • Update your Jan Aadhaar profile to include all family members correctly before applying for the certificate.

Couples under the Special Marriage Act face a mandatory 30-day public waiting period where notices are posted at the sub-registrar office, causing delays.

How to fix:

  • Under the SMA, you must wait 30 days after applying. Track notice publication at the sub-registrar office.

Confusion over whether to apply at the bride’s, groom’s, or marriage venue jurisdiction, particularly for inter-caste marriages.

How to fix:

  • Apply in the jurisdiction of the bride's residence, groom's residence, or where the marriage was solemnized.

The manual verification process by the Sub-Registrar can take longer than the stipulated 15–30 days, especially if witnesses are unavailable.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Sub-Registrar office with original documents and witnesses if verification takes more than 15 days.

The Pehchan portal, which handles registration, often faces performance issues due to high data volume, leading to suspensions in service.

How to fix:

  • Access the Pehchan portal during off-peak hours to avoid server timeouts.

Once an error is detected, correcting the surname, date of birth, or spelling on the certificate is difficult, and the Registrar can reject corrections if supporting documents are deemed insufficient.

How to fix:

  • Provide a sworn affidavit and strong supporting documents (like passport or 10th certificate) for any correction request.

In rural areas, reliance on intermediaries or e-Mitra operators who may not be fully trained leads to misinformation, higher costs, or delayed documentation.

How to fix:

  • Use official Rajasthan SSO online services or authorized e-Mitra kiosks only.
